truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 correctly identified 4 out of the 20 true vulnerabilities in the source code. While the audit demonstrates high accuracy on the identified flaws, many nuanced security risks (Format-String Injection, Logic Bombs, Race Conditions, Type-Confusion/Overflow Logic, and specific Memory Safety issues in the custom pool) were missed during the previous execution.

Items.Notes: Run 2 completely omitted the format-string injection in the log_debug_message function.
The integer overflow in the factorial function was not identified.
Run 2 failed to identify the Use-After-Free involving the scratch buffer in the asynchronous thread pool.
Run 2 correctly identified the command injection due to the use of system() on concatenated validator command strings.
Run 2 successfully identified the stack-allocated buffer overflow in evaluate_expression.
The write-read race condition on the volatile counter's old_value assignment was missed.
Run 2 did not flag the logic bomb embedded in the authentication logic overflow check.
The path traversal vulnerability in ConfigLoader was overlooked.
Run 2 accurately identified the heap overflow in the expression parsing stage due to the misuse of strcpy on a memory pool allocated buffer.
Run 2 successfully found the hardcoded administrative secret.
The choice of mt19937 and a small distribution for session tokens was not identified.
Thread safety issues with the static localtime timestamp cache were missed.
The insecure temporary file handling (TMPDIR expansion and shell command) was missed.
Persistence of authentication state/session tokens in memory was not noted.
Unhandled exceptions during std::stod parsing in the variable set command were missed.
Integer underflow logic during block splitting in the MemoryPool was missed.
The potentially unbounded recursion in parse_expression for balanced parentheses analysis was missed.
Data race in the high-concurrency result cache was missed.
The Time-of-Check Time-of-Use (TOCTOU) race condition in ConfigLoader was missed.
Division by zero in the calculate_route_score calculation was missed.
